title: matelea jaramilloi
intro: Matelea jaramilloi is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or milkweed family. It is native to Ecuador and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and wounds, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Matelea jaramilloi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its health benefits, such as reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
